# SPEC-QUIC-RFC9001 - QUIC TLS (RFC 9001)

## Purpose

Capture the QUIC TLS and packet-protection requirements defined by RFC 9001 as canonical spec-trace clauses.

## Scope

This specification covers the reviewed RFC 9001 extraction outputs and preserves the distinction between RFC 9001 packet-protection behavior and overlapping TLS or transport references.

## Context

RFC 9001 refines TLS behavior for QUIC packet protection and key updates. The assembly keeps RFC 9001 as a single RFC-level concern while reporting overlap with RFC 9000 and TLS references in generated review output.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S2-0001 Interpret uppercase BCP 14 keywords
Uppercase BCP 14 keywords in this document MUST be interpreted as described in RFC 2119 and RFC 8174 when they appear in all capitals.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §2 RFC9001-S2-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-2
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S2-0001.cs::RequirementStatement_UsesAllCapsBCP14Keywords

Notes:
- The sentence defines the interpretation rule for all-caps normative keywords in this document.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0001 Assume packet confidentiality
QUIC MUST assume responsibility for the confidentiality and integrity protection of packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- High-level packet protection responsibility stated in the introduction.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0002 Derive packet keys from TLS handshake
QUIC MUST use keys derived from a TLS handshake.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- The source sentence ties packet protection to keys derived from the TLS handshake.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0003 Carry handshake and alert messages directly
TLS handshake and alert messages MUST be carried directly over the QUIC transport.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- The source sentence contrasts this with TLS records over TCP.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0004 Replace the TLS record layer
QUIC transport MUST take over the responsibilities of the TLS record layer.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- The source sentence presents QUIC transport as replacing the TLS record layer for this traffic.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0005 Rely on TLS for security-critical functions
QUIC MUST rely on TLS for authentication and negotiation of parameters that are critical to security and performance.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B5-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- The source sentence states that TLS handles authentication and critical parameter negotiation.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0006 Use the TLS handshake
QUIC MUST use the TLS handshake.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B6-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- This is the first half of the cooperation sentence.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0007 Use QUIC-provided delivery services
TLS MUST use the reliability, ordered delivery, and record layer provided by QUIC.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B6-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- This is the second half of the cooperation sentence.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0008 Send TLS messages via QUIC
The TLS component MUST send and receive messages via the QUIC component.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B8-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- The source sentence also says QUIC provides TLS a reliable stream abstraction.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0009 Provide a reliable stream abstraction
QUIC MUST provide a reliable stream abstraction to TLS.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B8-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- This is the other half of the first interaction sentence.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0010 Provide TLS updates to QUIC
The TLS component MUST provide a series of updates to the QUIC component.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B9-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- The source sentence gives keys and state changes as examples of those updates.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0011 Do not use TLS Application Data records
QUIC applications that want to send data MUST NOT send it using TLS Application Data records.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B13-P6-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3

Notes:
- This is the negative form of the contrast with TLS over TCP.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0012 Send data as QUIC frames
QUIC applications that want to send data MUST send it as QUIC STREAM frames or other frame types carried in QUIC packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §3 RFC9001-S3-B13-P6-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-3
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicStreamFrameTests.cs::TryParseStreamFrame_ParsesOffsetsLengthsAndPayloadBytes
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicStreamFrameTests.cs::TryParseStreamFrame_UsesTheRemainderWhenLengthIsAbsent
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicStreamFrameTests.cs::TryFormatStreamFrame_RejectsInvalidTypesAndOffsetMismatches
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S3-0012.cs::TryFormatStreamFrame_RoundTripsAValidStreamFrame

Notes:
- The source sentence allows STREAM frames or other frame types; the open set is preserved.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0001 Carry handshake data in CRYPTO frames
QUIC MUST carry TLS handshake data in CRYPTO frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart3Tests.cs::TryParseCryptoFrame_ParsesAndFormatsAllFields

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0002 Define CRYPTO frame boundaries
Each CRYPTO frame MUST consist of a contiguous block of handshake data identified by an offset and length.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart3Tests.cs::TryParseCryptoFrame_AcceptsFramesThatExactlyReachTheStreamCeiling
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart3Tests.cs::TryParseCryptoFrame_ParsesAndFormatsAllFields
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicFrameCodecPart3Tests.cs::TryParseCryptoFrame_RejectsFramesThatExceedTheStreamCeiling

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0003 Package and encrypt CRYPTO frames
Those frames MUST be packaged into QUIC packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0004 Package and encrypt CRYPTO frames
Those frames MUST be encrypted under the current encryption level.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0005 Deliver handshake data reliably
Once TLS handshake data has been delivered to QUIC, QUIC MUST deliver it reliably.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B2-P1-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0006 Associate TLS-produced chunks with current keys
Each chunk of data that is produced by TLS MUST be associated with the set of keys that TLS is currently using.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B2-P1-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0007 Retransmit with the same keys
If QUIC needs to retransmit that data, it MUST use the same keys even if TLS has already updated to newer keys.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B2-P1-S5
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0008 Map encryption levels to packet number spaces
Each encryption level MUST correspond to a packet number space.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0009 Let packet number space determine frame semantics
The packet number space that is used MUST determine the semantics of frames.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0010 Indicate packet keys via packet type
QUIC MUST use the packet type to indicate which keys were used to protect a given packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S4-0011 Prefer coalesced packets
When packets of different types need to be sent, endpoints SHOULD use coalesced packets to send them in the same UDP datagram.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §4 RFC9001-S4-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-4

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0001 Protect packets with TLS-derived keys
QUIC MUST protect packets with keys derived from the TLS handshake.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §5 RFC9001-S5-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0002 Use the TLS-negotiated AEAD
QUIC MUST use the AEAD algorithm negotiated by TLS.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §5 RFC9001-S5-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0003 Leave Version Negotiation packets unprotected
Version Negotiation packets MUST NOT have cryptographic protection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §5 RFC9001-S5-B4-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-5

  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0003.cs::TryParseVersionNegotiation_RejectsOrdinaryProtectedLongHeaders

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0004 Protect Retry packets with AEAD_AES_128_GCM
Retry packets MUST use AEAD_AES_128_GCM to provide protection against accidental modification and limit the entities that can produce a valid Retry.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §5 RFC9001-S5-B5-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0005 Use AEAD_AES_128_GCM for Initial packets
Initial packets MUST use AEAD_AES_128_GCM.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §5 RFC9001-S5-B6-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0006 Derive Initial keys from the first client Initial
The keys for Initial packets MUST be derived from the Destination Connection ID field of the first Initial packet sent by the client.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §5 RFC9001-S5-B6-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0007 Protect all other packets cryptographically
All other packets MUST have strong cryptographic protections for confidentiality and integrity using keys and algorithms negotiated by TLS.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §5 RFC9001-S5-B7-P0-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0008 Apply the same protection process to Initial packets
The same packet protection process MUST be applied to Initial packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §5 RFC9001-S5-B8-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0009 Treat Initial packets as lacking confidentiality and integrity protection
Initial packets MUST NOT be considered to have confidentiality or integrity protection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §5 RFC9001-S5-B8-P3-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S5-0010 Use a fixed key for Retry packets
Retry packets MUST use a fixed key.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §5 RFC9001-S5-B8-P3-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-5

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0001 Allow key update after handshake confirmation
Once the handshake is confirmed, an endpoint MAY initiate a key update.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §6 RFC9001-S6-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0002 Identify packet protection keys with Key Phase
The Key Phase bit MUST indicate which packet protection keys are used to protect the packet.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0003 Initialize Key Phase to zero
The Key Phase bit MUST initially be set to 0 for the first set of 1-RTT packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §6 RFC9001-S6-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0004 Toggle Key Phase on each update
The Key Phase bit MUST be toggled to signal each subsequent key update.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §6 RFC9001-S6-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0005 Let Key Phase detect key changes
The Key Phase bit MUST allow a recipient to detect a change in keying material without needing to receive the first packet that triggered the change.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §6 RFC9001-S6-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0006 Update keys when Key Phase changes
An endpoint that notices a changed Key Phase bit MUST update keys.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §6 RFC9001-S6-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0007 Decrypt the packet with the changed Key Phase
An endpoint that notices a changed Key Phase bit MUST decrypt the packet that contains the changed value.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §6 RFC9001-S6-B4-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0008 Update both endpoints on key update
Initiating a key update MUST result in both endpoints updating keys.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §6 RFC9001-S6-B5-P4-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0009 Prohibit TLS KeyUpdate messages
Endpoints MUST NOT send a TLS KeyUpdate message.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §6 RFC9001-S6-B6-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S6-0010 Treat TLS KeyUpdate as a connection error
Endpoints MUST treat the receipt of a TLS KeyUpdate message as a connection error of type 0x010a, equivalent to a fatal TLS alert of unexpected_message.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §6 RFC9001-S6-B6-P5-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-6

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S7-0001 Use caution with unauthenticated Initial data
Implementations SHOULD use caution when relying on any data contained in Initial packets that is not otherwise authenticated.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §7 RFC9001-S7-B3-P2-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-7

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S7-0002 Fail on Handshake tampering
Any tampering with data carried in Handshake packets that requires modifying TLS handshake messages MUST cause the TLS handshake to fail.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §7 RFC9001-S7-B4-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-7

Notes:
- This restates the causal consequence in the source sentence as a normative invariant.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S8-0001 Carry QUIC transport parameters
The TLS handshake MUST carry values for QUIC transport parameters.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §8 RFC9001-S8-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-8
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S18-0002.cs::Fuzz_TransportParameters_RoundTripsRepresentativeValuesAndRejectsTruncation
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S18-0002.cs::TryFormatTransportParameters_WritesExactTupleSequence
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9000/REQ-QUIC-RFC9000-S18-0002.cs::TryParseTransportParameters_RoundTripsKnownFieldsAndPreferredAddress

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S8-0002 Authenticate QUIC transport parameters
The TLS handshake MUST authenticate values for QUIC transport parameters.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §8 RFC9001-S8-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-8

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S9-0001 Apply TLS security considerations to QUIC
All security considerations that apply to TLS MUST also apply to the use of TLS in QUIC.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §9 RFC9001-S9-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-9

Notes:
- This is a scope rule for security analysis rather than a packet-processing rule.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S10-0001 Register quic_transport_parameters at codepoint 57
IANA MUST register codepoint 57 (0x39) for the quic_transport_parameters extension in the TLS ExtensionType Values registry.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §10 RFC9001-S10-B2-P1-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-10
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S10-0001.cs::QuicTransportParametersCodec_ExposesTheRegisteredTlsExtensionMetadata

Notes:
- The source sentence reports the completed registration in past tense; this clause restates the registry action normatively.

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S10-0002 Mark Recommended as Yes
The Recommended column for the quic_transport_parameters extension MUST be marked Yes.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §10 RFC9001-S10-B3-P2-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-10

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-S10-0003 Include CH and EE in TLS 1.3 column
The TLS 1.3 column for the quic_transport_parameters extension MUST include CH (ClientHello) and EE (EncryptedExtensions).

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §10 RFC9001-S10-B3-P2-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-10

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SB-0001 Restrict larger packet-size-derived limits
Only endpoints that strictly limit packet size MAY use the larger confidentiality and integrity limits derived using the smaller packet size.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §B RFC9001-SB-B10-P2-S4
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-appendix.b
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SB-0001.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_UsesTheStrictPacketSizeProfileForGcm

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SB-0002 Require AEAD usage limits
Any AEAD that is used with QUIC MUST have limits on use that ensure that both confidentiality and integrity are preserved.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §B RFC9001-SB-B2-P1-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-appendix.b
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicAeadUsageLimitCalculatorTests.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_RespectsTheGcmPacketSizeThresholds
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicAeadUsageLimitCalculatorTests.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_UsesTheCcmPacketSizeThresholds
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SB-0002.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_RejectsUnsupportedPolicyCombinations

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP1P1-0001 Limit GCM confidentiality at 2^11 bytes
Endpoints that do not send packets larger than 2^11 bytes MUST NOT protect more than 2^28 packets in a single connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §B.1.1 RFC9001-SB.1.1-B6-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-appendix.b
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP1P1-0001.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_UsesTheStrictPacketSizeProfileForGcmConfidentiality

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP1P1-0002 Limit GCM confidentiality at 2^16 bytes
Endpoints that allow packets as large as 2^16 bytes MUST NOT protect more than 2^23 packets in a single connection.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §B.1.1 RFC9001-SB.1.1-B6-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-appendix.b
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP1P1-0002.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_UsesTheLargePacketSizeProfileForGcmConfidentiality

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP1P2-0001 Limit GCM integrity at 2^11 bytes
Endpoints that do not attempt to remove protection from packets larger than 2^11 bytes MUST NOT attempt to remove protection from more than 2^57 packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §B.1.2 RFC9001-SB.1.2-B6-P3-S1
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-appendix.b
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP1P2-0001.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_UsesTheStrictPacketSizeProfileForGcmIntegrity

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP1P2-0002 Limit GCM integrity at unrestricted size
Endpoints that do not restrict the size of processed packets MUST NOT attempt to remove protection from more than 2^52 packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §B.1.2 RFC9001-SB.1.2-B6-P3-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-appendix.b
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP1P2-0002.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_UsesTheLargePacketSizeProfileForGcmIntegrity

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP1P2-0003 Apply one GCM integrity limit to both functions
The same integrity limit SHOULD be applied to AEAD_AES_128_GCM and AEAD_AES_256_GCM.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §B.1.2 RFC9001-SB.1.2-B9-P5-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-appendix.b
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/QuicAeadUsageLimitCalculatorTests.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_UsesTheSameIntegrityLimitForAes128AndAes256Gcm
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP1P2-0003.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_UsesTheSameIntegrityLimitForAes128AndAes256Gcm

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP2-0001 Limit CCM at 2^11 bytes
Endpoints that limit packets to 2^11 bytes MUST have both confidentiality and integrity limits of 2^26.5 packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §B.2 RFC9001-SB.2-B12-P8-S2
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-appendix.b
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP2-0001.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_UsesTheStrictPacketSizeProfileForCcm

## REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP2-0002 Limit CCM at unrestricted packet size
Endpoints that do not restrict packet size MUST have a limit of 2^21.5 packets.

Trace:
- Satisfied By:
  - ARC-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Implemented By:
  - WI-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Verified By:
  - VER-QUIC-RFC9001-0001
- Source Refs:
  - RFC 9001 §B.2 RFC9001-SB.2-B12-P8-S3
  - https://www.rfc-editor.org/rfc/rfc9001.html#section-appendix.b
- Test Refs:
  - tests/Incursa.Quic.Tests/RequirementHomes/RFC9001/REQ-QUIC-RFC9001-SBP2-0002.cs::TryGetUsageLimits_UsesTheUnrestrictedPacketSizeProfileForCcm
